response:
An anonymous function checks if a number is prime. It uses modulus to test divisibility.  
A prime number has no divisors except one and itself. Status is confirmed through division.  
* * *  
The function returns true if no divisors are found. Status is determined by division results.  
A composite number has at least one divisor. Status is set by division outcome.  
* * *  
The modulus operator compares numbers against each other. Status is assigned based on remainder.  
A prime number passes all division tests. Status is final after all checks.
